name: Human Agreement
findings: ASF recall is 11.7% — well below the 70% target.
ASF precision is strong — extracted assumptions are relevant and on-topic.
F1 Score: 21.0% — harmonic mean of precision and recall.
recommendations: Major expansion needed in assumption extraction engine to capture implicit security assumptions.
Run Experiment 2 (Novel Discovery) to determine whether missed ground truth assumptions are actually false negatives or legitimate novel discoveries.

name: Novel Discovery
findings: Low novel discovery rate: 0.0% of ASF assumptions (0/41) are novel.
Novel discoveries by type: {}
recommendations: ASF may be overfitting to ground truth patterns rather than discovering genuinely new assumptions.

name: Differentiation

asf_unique_examples: No backup restore test performed in over 12 months.
Configuration drift alert fired but no action taken.
DR plan not tested in over 18 months.
Forensics readiness plan not updated in 2+ years.
Runbooks missing for 3 of 5 critical systems.
Architecture diagram does not match deployed infrastructure.
Network topology diagram outdated by 2+ years.
Incident response playbook not updated after major incident.
Software deployment without signed commit verification.

findings: ASF detects 100/100 scenarios — broadest coverage of any single tool type.
ASF uniquely detects 11 scenarios no other tool covers.
ASF shares 35 scenarios with vulnerability scanners, 7 with EDR, 68 with compliance tools, 32 with IAM tools.
Moderate differentiation: 11 uniquely detected scenarios. Moat exists but is narrow.
Access: ASF detects 20/20, unique in 0
Configuration: ASF detects 15/15, unique in 2
Dependency: ASF detects 5/5, unique in 3
Documentation: ASF detects 5/5, unique in 4
Governance: ASF detects 10/10, unique in 0
Identity: ASF detects 15/15, unique in 0
Network: ASF detects 15/15, unique in 0
Process: ASF detects 15/15, unique in 2

name: Executive Value

reports.title: Payroll Access Contradiction
reports.finding: Policy states only Finance employees may access payroll, but evidence shows 3 Engineering users have payroll access.
reports.risk: Unauthorized access to salary data — potential insider threat and GDPR violation.

reports.title: MFA Enrollment Gap
reports.finding: Policy requires MFA for all admin access, but 40% of admin users lack MFA enrollment.
reports.risk: Account takeover via credential compromise — most common initial access vector in breaches.

reports.title: Untested Backup Assumption
reports.finding: Policy states backups are performed daily, but no restore test has been performed in 18 months.
reports.risk: Data loss in disaster scenario — backups may be non-functional and organization is operating on an untested assumption.

reports.title: Network Isolation Contradiction
reports.finding: Policy states database servers are not internet accessible, but a production database has a public IP.
reports.risk: Direct data exposure to internet — database accessible to any attacker scanning the IP range.

reports.title: Access Review Not Performed
reports.finding: Policy requires quarterly access reviews, but no review conducted in current quarter.
reports.risk: Permission creep undetected — users accumulate access privileges over time without review.

reports.title: DR Plan Assumption
reports.finding: Disaster recovery plan exists but has not been tested in 24 months.
reports.risk: DR plan may be non-functional — organization assuming protection that does not exist.

reports.title: Service Account Over-Provisioned
reports.finding: Backup service account has admin privileges across all production systems.
reports.risk: Compromised backup service account provides unrestricted access to all production data.

reports.title: Configuration Drift
reports.finding: 3 production servers have configuration that differs from the security baseline.
reports.risk: Security controls may be ineffective on non-compliant systems — undetected drift undermines baseline.

reports.title: Dependency on Deprecated Service
reports.finding: Critical application depends on a service that is in end-of-life with no migration plan.
reports.risk: Unpatched vulnerabilities in EOL software — no security patches available.

reports.title: Documentation Out of Date
reports.finding: Network topology diagram describes architecture that was decommissioned 18 months ago.
reports.risk: Incident response teams operating on inaccurate information — delayed containment.

findings: Generated 10 executive-ready ASF findings (4 CRITICAL, 4 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM).
8/10 findings are designed to change decisions — the test of executive value.
70%+ of findings target decision-changing severity — strong signal for executive value.
EXPERIMENT REQUIRES HUMAN PARTICIPATION — Cannot be fully automated.
Recommended Protocol:
1. Present reports ER-001 through ER-010 to security managers/architects.
2. For each report, ask: 'Would this finding change a decision or investment?'
3. Ask: 'Would you pay for a tool that produced this finding automatically?'
4. Ask: 'Is this finding available from any existing tool you use?'
5. Score: YES = 1, MAYBE = 0.5, NO = 0
Target: average score >= 0.7 across all reviewers.
recommendations: Schedule 30-min sessions with 3+ security decision makers.
Document verbatim responses — qualitative feedback is as important as scores.
If executives consistently say 'we already know this', pivot to Experiment 2 (novel discovery) results.
If executives consistently say 'this is valuable but we have no budget', probe for what would unlock budget.
